title: UMH
text_content:
UNIVERSAL META HARNESS

0. SYSTEM DEFINITION
The Universal Meta Harness (UMH) is a:
stateful, event-driven, control-plane–oriented intelligence runtime that transforms multimodal signals into structured world representations, composes executable systems from those representations, routes execution across heterogeneous environments via standardized adapters, and continuously refines its internal world model and user models through governed feedback loops.

0.1 PURPOSE (ENFORCED CONSTRAINT)
Minimize human friction while maximizing human capability under real-world constraints.
All system decisions must optimize for this.

0.2 CORE IDENTITY
UMH is:
a control plane
a world-model engine
a second-brain memory system
a capability router
a governed execution runtime
a cross-device persistent cognition layer
UMH is NOT:
a chatbot
a UI
a tool wrapper
a SaaS product

0.3 HARD INVARIANTS
Control Plane Exclusivity All signals, decisions, and actions must pass through the control plane.
Adapter Isolation No external system is accessed directly.
Typed Contracts Only All modules communicate via explicit protocols.
Single Execution Spine Only one execution path exists.
Memory Discipline All memory writes go through memory subsystem.
Governance First No execution without authorization.
Environment Explicitness Every action declares its execution environment.

1. DISTRIBUTION & INSTALLATION
UMH must be installable like a harness.
1.1 Distribution Layer
distribution/
  installer.py
  updater.py
  package_manager.py
  platform_detector.py
  dependency_resolver.py
1.2 Installation Flow
Install UMH
→ detect OS/device
→ install runtime + dependencies
→ initialize user instance
→ run onboarding
→ enter active runtime

2. USER INSTANTIATION
Each installation creates a User Instance.
UserInstance {
  user_id
  devices
  permissions
  adapters
  memory_space
  world_model
  profiles
  preferences
  governance_policy
  boot_sequences
  work_modes
}
This is not optional — the harness is stateful per user.

3. ONBOARDING / FIRST BOOT
3.1 Onboarding System
onboarding/
  setup_wizard.py
  device_linking.py
  permission_config.py
  adapter_setup.py
  first_boot.py
3.2 First Boot Sequence
Initialize user
→ detect devices
→ register environments
→ configure adapters
→ set governance defaults
→ initialize memory + world model
→ create workstation profile
→ generate initial context
→ run first boot sequence

4. CORE ARCHITECTURE
universal_meta_harness/
  core/
  ontology/
  protocols/
  interpretation/
  decomposition/
  world_model/
  memory/
  profiles/
  planning/
  composition/
  completeness/
  quality/
  capabilities/
  adapters/
  execution/
  environments/
  perception/
  actuation/
  presence/
  runtime/
  workstation/
  interface/
  governance/
  observability/
  learning/
  registry/
  storage/
  security/
  distribution/
  onboarding/
  tests/

5. ONTOLOGY (FOUNDATION)
5.1 Primitives
state
change
constraint
resource
time
signal
feedback
goal
action
outcome
5.2 Laws
causality
feedback
compounding
entropy
emergence
constraints
equilibrium
temporal dependency
These govern all reasoning.

6. SIGNAL INTERPRETATION SYSTEM
The system does NOT process text — it processes signals.
6.1 Input Types
text
voice
video
system events
behavioral patterns
environment state

6.2 Decomposition Principle (CRITICAL)
Every user input is decomposed into:
tokens → grammar → semantics → concepts → primitives → relationships → constraints → context
Key Rule
Every word, structure, and omission carries signal.

6.3 Linguistic Signal Extraction
The system extracts:
vocabulary level
communication style
clarity vs ambiguity
intention gap
focus areas
avoidance patterns
emotional tone
mental model indicators
This updates the User Model continuously.

7. DECOMPOSITION ENGINE
decomposition/
  lexical_parser.py
  grammar_analyzer.py
  concept_extractor.py
  entity_extractor.py
  primitive_mapper.py
  relationship_builder.py
  context_binder.py

7.1 Concept Representation
ConceptNode {
  id
  term
  definition
  type
  primitive_mapping
  relationships
  context_usage
  canonical_meaning
  instance_meaning
}

7.2 Important Clarification
Not every word becomes a stored node immediately.
Instead:
Only meaningful concepts are promoted into structured memory.

8. BREADTH → DEPTH REASONING
8.1 Principle
Expand context breadth-first.
Then deepen selectively based on relevance.

8.2 Execution
Step 1 — Breadth Expansion
Identify ALL relevant domains, entities, processes.
Step 2 — Completeness Check
Ensure no required system component is missing.
Step 3 — Depth Expansion
Deepen specific areas based on:
importance
uncertainty
impact

8.3 Example
User: “Build a CRM for construction”
Breadth ensures:
sales
estimating
contracts
projects
crew
materials
scheduling
invoicing
collections
fulfillment
reporting
feedback
Depth focuses on:
lead pipeline
job tracking
crew scheduling

9. COMPLETENESS ENGINE
Ensures no system is incomplete.
completeness/
  breadth_expander.py
  domain_map.py
  omission_detector.py
  coverage_checker.py
  required_slots.py

9.1 System Slots
Every system must include:
input
processing
output
feedback
constraints
failure handling
optimization loop

10. WORLD MODEL
10.1 Structure
Graph-based:
entities
relationships
state
time
uncertainty

10.2 Tracks
user
environment
devices
goals
constraints
ongoing tasks
interactions

10.3 Uncertainty
Every fact includes:
confidence
source
timestamp

11. MEMORY SYSTEM (SECOND BRAIN)
Types
working
episodic
semantic
instance
canonical
behavioral

Capabilities
recall
search
summarize
cross-link
timeline replay

Core Idea
UMH becomes the user’s externalized cognition layer.

12. PROFILE / DOSSIER SYSTEM
profiles/
  user_profile.py
  contact_profile.py
  client_profile.py
  entity_profile.py
  dossier_builder.py

Profile Contents
identity
behavior
preferences
relationships
history
communication style
goals
risks

13. CAPABILITY SYSTEM
Everything is abstracted as:
Capability {
  inputs
  outputs
  cost
  latency
  reliability
  environment
}

14. ADAPTER SYSTEM
Adapters implement:
connect()
validate()
execute()
observe()
disconnect()

15. EXECUTION SYSTEM
Single execution spine:
plan → queue → DAG → execution → result

16. ENVIRONMENTS
Execution contexts:
local device
mobile
VPS
cloud
browser
sandbox
simulation

17. PERCEPTION SYSTEM
Inputs:
webcam
audio
screen
sensors

18. ACTUATION SYSTEM
Outputs:
OS control
browser automation
device control
API actions

19. PRESENCE SYSTEM
Supports:
live conversations
meetings
interruptions

20. WORKSTATION (JARVIS)
WorkstationProfile
WorkstationProfile {
  devices
  modes
  boot_sequences
  permissions
}

Boot Sequences
Examples:
Developer Mode
Research Mode
Command Center
Overnight Mode

Continuity
User leaves
→ system continues
→ user returns
→ summary + resume

21. GOVERNANCE
Authority levels:
autonomous
notify
approve
escalate
deny

22. QUALITY / EXCELLENCE ENGINE
quality/
  excellence_gate.py
  completeness_validator.py
  benchmark_engine.py

Rule
Never produce the first acceptable answer.
Produce the best possible answer under constraints.

23. LEARNING SYSTEM
Outcome → Feedback → Pattern → Compression → Update

24. FINAL SYSTEM DEFINITION
Universal Meta Harness =
  control plane
  + ontology
  + interpretation
  + decomposition
  + world model
  + second-brain memory
  + profiles
  + planning
  + composition
  + completeness engine
  + quality engine
  + capability system
  + adapter system
  + execution spine
  + environments
  + perception
  + actuation
  + presence
  + workstation
  + runtime
  + governance
  + observability
  + learning
  + distribution
  + onboarding

FINAL TRUTH
This system:
does not respond to the user.
It understands the user.
It models reality.
It composes systems.
It executes actions.
It learns continuously.
It becomes the user’s extended cognition.
